extracted_rules.rule_name: MEV — Minimum Effective Volume per muscle group
extracted_rules.condition: If the goal is to maintain muscle mass and strength during a period of reduced training
extracted_rules.action: Prescribe at least MEV sets per muscle group per week; below MEV, detraining occurs
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: MEV: ~8-10 sets/week per muscle group (maintenance threshold); individual range: 6-12 sets depending on training history
extracted_rules.source: Israetel et al., Scientific Principles of Hypertrophy Training; meta-regression: weekly volume and hypertrophy

extracted_rules.rule_name: MAV — Maximum Adaptive Volume per muscle group
extracted_rules.condition: If the goal is hypertrophy and athlete is in an accumulation block
extracted_rules.action: Target MAV range for primary muscle groups; volume within MAV produces the best hypertrophy return-on-investment
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: MAV: ~15-20 sets/week per muscle group; individual range: 12-22 sets; peak hypertrophy response in this window
extracted_rules.source: Israetel et al., Scientific Principles of Hypertrophy Training; Schoenfeld et al. 2017 dose-response

extracted_rules.rule_name: MRV — Maximum Recoverable Volume per muscle group
extracted_rules.condition: When an athlete approaches or exceeds MRV for any muscle group
extracted_rules.action: Reduce volume back to MAV or MEV; above MRV, fatigue exceeds adaptation and injury risk rises significantly
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: MRV: ~25+ sets/week per muscle group (highly individual); at MRV: diminishing returns; above MRV: detraining + injury risk
extracted_rules.source: Israetel et al., Scientific Principles of Hypertrophy Training

extracted_rules.rule_name: DUP — Daily Undulating Periodization weekly structure
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete trains the same muscle group ≥3 times per week
extracted_rules.action: Vary rep ranges across sessions (DUP); do not repeat same rep range within the same muscle group on consecutive sessions
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: DUP weekly template: Session 1 = 3-5 reps (strength); Session 2 = 8-12 reps (hypertrophy); Session 3 = 15-20 reps (endurance/metabolic); rotate weekly
extracted_rules.source: DUP research: Zourdos et al. 2016; Colquhoun et al. 2017

extracted_rules.rule_name: RPE scale — Reps In Reserve (RIR) method
extracted_rules.condition: When prescribing training intensity using RPE
extracted_rules.action: Use RIR-based RPE scale; prescribe sets at RPE 7-9 for hypertrophy work; RPE 9-10 reserved for strength testing or AMRAP sets
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: RPE 6 = 4 RIR; RPE 7 = 3 RIR; RPE 8 = 2 RIR; RPE 9 = 1 RIR; RPE 10 = maximal (0 RIR); hypertrophy target: RPE 7-9
extracted_rules.source: Zourdos et al. 2016 RIR-based RPE validation; Helms et al. 2016

extracted_rules.rule_name: Progressive overload — when to add load
extracted_rules.condition: When deciding whether to increase load on a given exercise
extracted_rules.action: Add load only when the top working set is completed at RPE ≤7 for 2 consecutive sessions; do not increase load if RPE is ≥8
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Load increase trigger: top set RPE ≤7 for 2 consecutive sessions; load increment: 2.5-5 kg for upper body, 5-10 kg for lower body
extracted_rules.source: Progressive overload consensus; RIR-based progression (Helms et al. 2016)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Drop-set training for hypertrophy and time efficiency
extracted_rules.condition: If hypertrophy is the goal and training time is limited
extracted_rules.action: Use drop-sets to achieve equivalent hypertrophic stimulus in less time than traditional sets
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Drop-sets: 2-3 drops of 20-25% load per set; equivalent or superior hypertrophy vs. traditional sets at matched volume; time reduction: ~40%
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: acute and chronic effects of drop-set training (original lifting_coach_volume_rules source)

extracted_rules.condition: If heavy loading is contraindicated (injury, post-surgery) and hypertrophy or strength maintenance is needed
extracted_rules.action: Apply BFR with 20-30% 1RM; produces hypertrophy and strength gains comparable to high-intensity training
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: BFR load: 20-30% 1RM; cuff pressure: 50-80% arterial occlusion pressure; sets: 4-5 per exercise; reps: 15-30; rest: 30-60s
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: BFR interval training on hypertrophy and strength (original lifting_coach_volume_rules source)

extracted_rules.condition: When designing weekly volume for a hypertrophy block
extracted_rules.action: Higher weekly set volume produces greater hypertrophy up to MRV; increase sets gradually across a mesocycle
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Dose-response: each additional set/week up to MRV adds measurable hypertrophy; recommended progression: +2 sets/week per muscle group across 4-week mesocycle
extracted_rules.source: Meta-regression: weekly volume and muscle hypertrophy/strength (original lifting_coach_volume_rules source)

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is performing resistance training and targeting lean mass gain
extracted_rules.action: Recommend creatine monohydrate supplementation; well-established for lean mass improvement; benefits are greater in novice to intermediate lifters
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Dose: 3-5g/day creatine monohydrate; loading phase optional (20g/day × 5 days); no significant difference between novice and experienced for lean mass; timing flexible
extracted_rules.source: Dose-response meta-analysis: creatine and resistance training in novice vs experienced lifters (original source)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Velocity-based training and eccentric overload for maximal strength
extracted_rules.condition: If the primary goal is maximal strength (1RM) improvement
extracted_rules.action: Include velocity-based training (VBT) or eccentric overload methods; these provide greater strength gains than traditional loading alone
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: VBT: target bar velocity 0.5-0.8 m/s for strength; eccentric overload: 120-130% concentric 1RM; g=0.351 strength benefit vs traditional
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: advanced resistance training systems for strength (original lifting_coach_volume_rules source)

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete wants an alternative to drop-sets for hypertrophy
extracted_rules.action: Use rest-pause training as a time-efficient hypertrophy method; modest advantage over traditional sets
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Rest-pause: perform set to near-failure, rest 15-30s, extend set for additional reps; repeat 2-3 times; modest hypertrophy advantage vs. traditional sets
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: advanced resistance training systems (original lifting_coach_volume_rules source)
